expectations.text: ProGuard rules are explicitly identified as a hidden consumer that must be updated
expectations.evidence: 'ProGuard/R8 keep rules reference these classes by name. If files are renamed or deleted without updating ProGuard rules, the release build will silently strip them, causing runtime crashes in production.' Listed as the first hidden consumer in the risk flags section.

expectations.text: Agent recommends extracting networking code to a dedicated Gradle module as preparation
expectations.evidence: Option A (recommended) preparation: 'Extract the 6 network files into a new :network Gradle module. Introduce a technology-neutral interface for each service.' Rationale includes 'Isolating first limits blast radius to the :network module boundary'.

expectations.text: At least one strategy option includes module isolation as a named preparation step
expectations.evidence: Option A is titled 'Branch by Abstraction + Module Isolation (recommended)' with module extraction as the first preparation step. Option B (Parallel in :app) explicitly contrasts by *not* including isolation — which also demonstrates the agent understands isolation as a distinct choice.

expectations.text: Agent presents options and waits for user choice before starting any work
expectations.evidence: 'Waiting for your choice (A, B, or C) before proceeding to Phase 2.' Response ends there. Additionally: '>5 files or module restructuring... I will generate a migration-checklist.md with one row per unit and present it for your approval before any code changes begin.'

claims.claim: OkHttp interceptors and error handling conventions are also hidden consumers
claims.evidence: Old-skill adds two additional hidden consumers beyond ProGuard: 'OkHttp interceptors (auth token injection, certificate pinning)' and 'Error handling conventions (HttpException vs ResponseException)'. These are valuable observations not required by the assertions — shows the old skill also has strong hidden consumer analysis.

eval_feedback.overall: Both runs pass all assertions. Old-skill adds extra hidden consumers (interceptors, error handling) beyond ProGuard. With-skill gives a more prominent treatment to ProGuard as 'critical' with a dedicated post-migration note. Qualitatively comparable.
